toasts.failedAutoExecute: Failed to continue auto-execution of the current chapter range.

metric.recentAutoProgressHint: Recent projects actively running auto-director or auto-execution in the background.
metric.recentActionRequiredHint: Recent projects waiting for review, failed, or cancelled that require your next step.
metric.recentExecutionReadyHint: Recent projects ready for writing; you can directly proceed to chapter production.
metric.backgroundFailedTasksHint: Total count of failed tasks in the task center, which can be managed later.

recommendation.titleHasNovels: Want to start your next book quickly? Let AI auto-direct first.
recommendation.titleNoNovels: First time? Let AI auto-direct guide you to open a book.
recommendation.description: You only need to provide a rough idea, and AI will generate direction schemes, title packages, and book setup first, stopping at key stages to wait for your approval. You don't need to know the whole structure from the beginning.
recommendation.suitability1: Suitable when you haven't fully figured out the theme, selling points, and first 30 chapters' promise
recommendation.suitability2: Also suitable to quickly spin up a new project

recentProject.title: Continue Recent Project
recentProject.description: The home page directly takes you back to the most relevant book to continue writing.
recentProject.errorLoading: Failed to read project list; cannot recommend the next step on home page.
recentProject.emptyTitle: You haven't started any novel projects yet. For the first time, it's recommended to go with AI Auto-Direct, which sets up your direction and preparation.

quickOps.title: Quick Actions
quickOps.description: Common entry points and the easiest ways to start a book are grouped together.

recentProjectsList.title: Recent Projects
recentProjectsList.description: Displays not only titles but also current stages and resumption entry points.
recentProjectsList.errorLoading: Failed to load recent projects. Please try again later.
recentProjectsList.emptyList: No novel projects yet. Start by creating a new novel.

basicInfo.tipsDescription: It is recommended to first figure out who this book is for, how it attracts readers, and what promises are made in the first 30 chapters, before adjusting writing mode, world boundary, and style choices. The settings here directly affect story planning, volume progression, and text generation.
basicInfo.sectionOrientationDescription: Define what kind of book this is, and whether it starts from scratch or continues from existing content.
basicInfo.leadSummaryPlaceholder: Use 2-4 sentences to describe the protagonist, core conflict, and key selling points.
basicInfo.novelLanguageHint: The language the AI uses when generating chapters, outlines, and character profiles.
basicInfo.genreDiffTipTitle: Difference Between Genre Base and Story Mode
basicInfo.genreDiffTipText: Genre Base answers 'what kind of book is this' (e.g., cultivation, urban, alternate history); Story Mode answers 'how does the story progress and deliver' (e.g., system-driven, invincible, slice-of-life).
basicInfo.defaultChapterLengthHint: Recommended to set 2500-3500 first. You can adjust per chapter later.
basicInfo.estimatedChapterCountHint: Used as a reference for outline, beats, and pipelines. Can be adjusted later.
basicInfo.primaryStoryModePlaceholder: Do not set Primary Story Mode
basicInfo.secondaryStoryModePlaceholder: Do not overlay Secondary Story Mode
basicInfo.advancedSectionTitle: Narrative Experience & AI Collaboration Advanced Settings
basicInfo.advancedSectionDescription: These affect style and AI automation, but do not need immediate decisions.
basicInfo.worldSampleDescription: This is only for initialization reference. Please import, generate, or customize the actual world in the card above.
basicInfo.narrativeExperienceDescription: These define how readers perceive the work and affect the tone, density, and pacing of chapter generation.
basicInfo.aiCollaborationDescription: Defines how tasks are split between you and AI, and the level of automation.
basicInfo.resourceReadyScoreHint: 0 means just starting, 100 means settings, characters, and plans are fully ready.
basicInfo.postGenerationStyleReviewHint: When enabled, generated text is checked for AI artifacts and polished if issues are found.
basicInfo.continuationDescription: Continuation mode requires setting the upstream source, so it is expanded by default.
basicInfo.projectStatusDescription: Used for project management and pipeline triggers, not immediate decisions.
basicInfo.productionProgressDescription: Used for progress management. Can be adjusted per phase later.
basicInfo.continuationSourceMissingError: Continuation mode requires a selected upstream source before saving.
basicInfo.continuationAnalysisSectionMissingError: Book analysis sections require at least one target chapter selected.
